title: A detailed protocol for protein-protein or protein-RNA interaction screening using rec-YnH
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4oiaw","text":"Knowing which proteins and RNAs directly interact is essential for understanding cellular mechanisms. Unfortunately, discovering such interactions is costly and often unreliable. To overcome these limitations, we developed rec-YnH, a new yeast two- and three-hybrid-based \"screening pipeline\":http://www.nature.com/protocolexchange/system/uploads/7059/original/Figure_1.pdf?1533558364 capable of detecting interactions within protein libraries or between protein libraries and RNA fragment pools. rec-YnH combines batch cloning and transformation with intracellular homologous recombination to generate bait-prey fusion libraries. By developing interaction selection in liquid-gels and using an ORF sequence-based readout of interactions via next-generation sequencing, we eliminate laborious plating and barcoding steps required by existing methods. We use rec-Y2H to simultaneously map interactions of protein domains and reveal novel putative interactors of PAR proteins. We further employ rec-Y2H to predict the architecture of published coprecipitated complexes. Finally, we use rec-Y3H to map interactions between multiple RNA-binding proteins and RNAs – the first time interactions between protein and RNA pools are simultaneously detected.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"swv6f","text":"Revealing which proteins, protein domains and RNAs undergo direct, physical interactions is an essential prerequisite for understanding the principles by which cellular machines operate. Assays that can detect such interactions are in high demand as they function both to generate system-level views of cellular coordination but also to generate hypothesis for subsequent mechanistic studies. Yeast two and three hybrid (Y2H, Y3H) screens, allow to detect direct protein-protein and protein-RNA interactions under physiological conditions. The Y2H assay was enhanced significantly in the past. Coupling the readout to next-generation sequencing (NGS) and multiplexed generation of genetically fused bait-prey libraries resulted in increased assay sensitivity and reproducibility while allowing screening many baits against many preys simultaneously. Improvements of Y3H, however, mostly focused on optimized RNA binding and presentation by linker RNA engineering and selection of stronger MS2-binding proteins. To date, there is no method available allowing the many-by-many direct interaction detection of full-length RBPs and RNA-fragments.\r","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"exfxz","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ogusm","text":"With the development of rec-YnH, we aimed at creating \"one assay\":http://www.nature.com/protocolexchange/system/uploads/7013/original/DetailedProtocolrec-YnH.pdf?1533133123 that further advances and integrates past developments in Y2H and Y3H technologies into a single, new assay pipeline allowing screening of protein libraries against protein libraries or RNA fragment libraries. At the same time, we focused on keeping the assay workflow as simple as possible to make it affordable and doable for the standard biomedical research lab. The assay pipeline combines batch-cloning and transformation with intracellular homologous recombination, liquid gel culturing, yeast two or three-hybrid based interaction detection and read out by next-generation-sequencing. This eliminates barcoding, arraying and plating steps needed for available methods. Three compatible vectors allow high-throughput interaction screening within protein libraries and, for the first time, between protein and RNA libraries. We demonstrate that the same assay pipeline can be used for rec-Y2H and rec-Y3H screening and thus create an assay for both, many-by-many protein-protein or protein-RNA interaction detection.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":142,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Please see attached "Detailed Protocol": Details for a full protocol.
Please see Figure 1 for a "Workflow Overview": Workflow Overview
Batch Gateway cloning of ORF library into pDEST vectors
Competent yeast
Linearization
Batch yeast transformation
Growth in liquid-gel selection media
Harvest yeast
Yeast library DNA isolation
Covaris shearing
Circularisation
R1/R2 PCR
P5/P7 PCR
Library quality control and paired-end MiSeq
Analysis
Batch Gateway cloning of ORF library into pDEST vectors
Batch Gateway cloning of ORF library into pDEST vectors - Prepare pool of pENTR-ORF clones at 10 nM - Prepare pDEST for Bait and Prey at 10 nM - Set up LR reactions
Prepare competent yeast - Streak Y2HGold on a YPDA plate
Batch Gateway cloning of ORF library into pDEST vectors - Continue LR reaction - Transform LR reaction into NEB stable cells - Plate on LB-Agar with corresponding antibiotic
Batch Gateway cloning of ORF library into pDEST vectors - Colony counting - Harvest colonies from LB-Agar plates - Qiaprep DNA prep of Bait and Prey pDEST-ORF pool
Competent yeast - Grow 1 colony of Y2HGold during 8 hours - Transfer to 50 ml YPDA media - Grow overnight
Linearization - Digest Bait and Prey pDEST-ORF pool with I-SceI/I-CeuI - Heat inactivation
Competent yeast - Transfer yeast to 100 ml YPDA - Grow yeast to OD 0.5 - Wash cells - Resuspend in TE/LiAc

Batch yeast transformation - Prepare Seaprep liquid-gel selection media - Multiple transformations of yeast with linear Bait and Prey pDEST-ORF pool - Pool together all transformations - Plate dilutions for colony counting
Growth in selection media - Add transformations to liquid-gel selection media - 1 hour on ice - Grow at 30ºC for 60 hours
Harvest yeast - Colony counting - Centrifuge liquid-gel selection media with yeast colonies - Wash and resuspend in PBS - OD660 to calculate concentration of cells
Yeast library DNA isolation - For each selection media, Zymoprep™ Yeast Plasmid Miniprep of recombined Bait and Prey fragments
Covaris shearing - Shear DNA to 1,500-bp fragments
Circularisation - End repair - Mini Elute DNA purification - Ligation - Heat inactivation
R1/R2 PCR - 12 PCR cycles with Q5 polymerase - Purification with AMPure beads
P5/P7 PCR - 12 PCR cycles with Q5 polymerase and NEBNext® Multiplex Oligos for Illumina - Purification with AMPure beads
Paired-end sequencing - Library quality control - MiSeq run
Analysis

PARTS OF THE PROTOCOL Part I. Bait and Prey library preparation : Day 1-3 Part II. Bait and Prey library fusion & Selection in liquid gel: Day 3-7 Part III. NGS library preparation: Day 7 Part IV. Paired-end sequencing: Day 8-9
None

rec-YnH enables simultaneous many-by-many detection of direct protein–protein and protein–RNA interactions by Jae-Seong Yang, Mireia Garriga-Canut, Nele Link, Carlo Carolis, Katrina Broadbent, Violeta Beltran-Sastre, Luis Serrano, and Sebastian P. Maurer published in Nature Communications on 14 Sep, 2018 https://dx.doi.org/10.1038/s41467-018-06128-x
